Title: Metabolic engineering of astaxanthin-rich maize and its use in the production of biofortified eggs

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hi-II; backcrossed to Z58, C7-2, NM28, NF28
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PR5SGPA seed-specific bidirectional promoter driving LCY-e RNAi; seed-specific, bidirectional
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown in transgenic stacks; PCR/RT-PCR confirmation; HPLC metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: normally active in carotenoid biosynthesis; downregulated in transgenic seeds by RNAi
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant pigment; red yolk biofortification through feed transfer
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: intended to reduce competing lutein branch and favor β-carotene flux toward astaxanthin, though not clearly superior to non-RNAi construct in event comparison
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LCY-e was knocked down by RNAi to limit lycopene conversion to α-carotene/lutein branch. 2. This was designed to favor the β-carotene branch for astaxanthin biosynthesis. 3. Productive astaxanthin-rich events were generated with the RNAi construct, but superiority over the non-RNAi construct was not consistently demonstrated in the reported events.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may alter native lutein branch flux
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful branch-control element for diverting carotenoid flux toward β-branch in astaxanthin engineering, but benefit should be empirically tested by event
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: treated here as a common gene because the paper uses RNAi branch suppression rather than enzyme overexpression as the main function

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: P2R5SGPA seed-specific bidirectional promoter; seed-specific, bidirectional
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression in combinatorial stacks; HPLC metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cytochrome P450 carotenoid oxygenase/ketolase pathway component
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in seed under transgene promoter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: alone with XdCrtR failed to support astaxanthin production in maize and reduced accumulation when combined with productive modules
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XdCrtS/XdCrtR from yeast did not produce astaxanthin when expressed with upstream CP module in maize. 2. Co-expression with BZ reduced astaxanthin accumulation substantially. 3. The yeast SR route appears incompatible or inhibitory in maize seeds.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: XdCrtR; ZmPSY1; PaCrtI; CrBKT; HpCrtZ; AdCBFD1; AdHBFD1
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: competes for precursors and may depress astaxanthin yield
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: not recommended for maize astaxanthin engineering based on negative or null effects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: discussion suggests unique P450-based pathway less efficient in maize

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: P2R5SGPA seed-specific bidirectional promoter; seed-specific, bidirectional
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression in combinatorial stacks; HPLC metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in seed under transgene promoter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required partner of XdCrtS in yeast pathway but ineffective or inhibitory for astaxanthin production in maize
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XdCrtR was tested with XdCrtS as the yeast SR module. 2. The SR module failed to produce astaxanthin in maize. 3. SR inclusion lowered astaxanthin accumulation in productive module combinations.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: XdCrtS; ZmPSY1; PaCrtI; CrBKT; HpCrtZ; AdCBFD1; AdHBFD1
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: competes for precursor use and lowers product accumulation in combined stacks
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: not suitable for maize astaxanthin biofortification according to this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: acts in yeast-specific P450-based astaxanthin route; paper infers species-context incompatibility

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hi-II versus Z58 yellow endosperm background
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: multiple copies in Hi-II/white endosperm background; low copy in Z58
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: high CCD1 background likely degrades carotenoids and interferes with astaxanthin accumulation; low-copy yellow endosperm backgrounds improve accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD1 genes degrade carotenoids such as lycopene, β-carotene and zeaxanthin. 2. Hi-II contains multiple CCD1 copies that interfere with endosperm carotenoid accumulation. 3. Crossing to yellow-endosperm line Z58 with low CCD1 copy number improved carotenoid/astaxanthin accumulation context.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: genetic background of white versus yellow endosperm maize
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: choose low-CCD1 yellow-endosperm germplasm as recurrent background for carotenoid biofortification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: background determinant rather than transformed focal transgene

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hi-II; backcrossed to Z58, C7-2, NM28, NF28
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PR5SGPA/P2R5SGPA seed-specific bidirectional promoter; seed-specific, bidirectional
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation; RT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed kernel including embryo, aleurone layer, and endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant pigment; feed and food colorant; accumulates in egg yolk after feeding
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: upstream precursor supply to β-carotene/astaxanthin branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses geranylgeranyl diphosphate to form phytoene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in whole seed under seed-specific bidirectional promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes precursor supply for elevated astaxanthin accumulation in engineered maize seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPSY1 was included in all constructs to broaden precursor supply. 2. It catalyzes geranylgeranyl diphosphate to phytoene upstream of lycopene formation. 3. Its co-expression with downstream ketolase/hydroxylase modules supported high astaxanthin accumulation in seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development; yellow endosperm background enhances accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PaCrtI; CrBKT; HpCrtZ; AdCBFD1; AdHBFD1; XdCrtS; XdCrtR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful as an upstream precursor-supply gene in multigene stacks for astaxanthin biofortification, especially with yellow-endosperm germplasm and seed-wide expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: native maize gene; not individually dissected apart from inclusion in all productive constructs

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hi-II; backcrossed to Z58, C7-2, NM28, NF28
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PR5SGPA/P2R5SGPA seed-specific bidirectional promoter; seed-specific, bidirectional
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; RT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed kernel including embryo, aleurone layer, and endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant pigment; feed and food colorant
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: upstream precursor supply to β-carotene/astaxanthin branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in whole seed under seed-specific bidirectional promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increases lycopene precursor availability for astaxanthin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaCrtI was used in all constructs to convert phytoene to lycopene. 2. Together with ZmPSY1 it broadened precursor source for astaxanthin production. 3. Its inclusion supported high astaxanthin accumulation in maize seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZmPSY1; CrBKT; HpCrtZ; AdCBFD1; AdHBFD1; XdCrtS; XdCrtR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: key upstream module for carotenoid precursor enhancement in cereal astaxanthin engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: codon-optimized and fused to maize plastid transit peptide

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hi-II; backcrossed to Z58, C7-2, NM28, NF28
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PR5SGPA/P2R5SGPA seed-specific bidirectional promoter; seed-specific, bidirectional
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression in multigene stacks; comparison among gene-pair combinations; HPLC and MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: ketolated carotenoid intermediates leading to astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed kernel including embryo, aleurone layer, and endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant pigment; red coloration; feed biofortification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces keto groups into β-rings of β-carotene or hydroxylated intermediates to generate ketocarotenoid intermediates toward astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and immediate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point supported by highest-yielding BZ module among tested ketolase/hydroxylase pairs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in whole seed under seed-specific bidirectional promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly promotes astaxanthin accumulation; BZ module gave the highest astaxanthin level (111.82 mg/kg DW)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrBKT paired with HpCrtZ enabled robust astaxanthin biosynthesis in maize seeds. 2. The BZ module outperformed the CH module and far exceeded SR-containing combinations. 3. Co-expression with SR reduced astaxanthin output, indicating CrBKT/HpCrtZ is the most effective sink module tested.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression; yellow endosperm background improves output
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: very high carotenoid flux to astaxanthin may reduce ABA precursor availability during regeneration when combined with additional modules
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: best-performing ketolase for cereal astaxanthin engineering in this study; recommended as core sink module for high-yield astaxanthin maize
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: all-E, 9Z and 13Z astaxanthin isomers accumulated in BZ-containing lines

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hi-II; backcrossed to Z58, C7-2, NM28, NF28
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PR5SGPA/P2R5SGPA seed-specific bidirectional promoter; seed-specific, bidirectional
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression in multigene stacks; comparison among gene-pair combinations; HPLC and MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: hydroxylated carotenoid intermediates leading to astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed kernel including embryo, aleurone layer, and endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant pigment; red coloration; feed biofortification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates β-rings of β-carotene or ketolated intermediates to generate hydroxylated intermediates toward astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and immediate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key component of best-performing sink module with CrBKT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in whole seed under seed-specific bidirectional promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes astaxanthin accumulation when paired with CrBKT; contributes to highest astaxanthin levels observed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HpCrtZ with CrBKT formed the highest-efficiency downstream module for astaxanthin synthesis. 2. BZ-containing constructs produced substantial all-E, 9Z and 13Z astaxanthin. 3. Addition of SR reduced output, highlighting the effectiveness of HpCrtZ/CrBKT relative to alternative combinations.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression; yellow endosperm background improves output
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: preferred hydroxylase partner for high-level astaxanthin engineering in maize seeds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: used as algal benchmark hydroxylase in comparative module testing

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: P2R5SGPA seed-specific bidirectional promoter; seed-specific, bidirectional
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression in multigene stacks; comparison among gene-pair combinations; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: carotenoid β-ring 4-dehydrogenase family component (CBFD1-associated hydroxylase route)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: carotenoid biosynthetic enzyme from Adonis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: participates with AdHBFD1 in a higher-plant pathway converting carotene intermediates toward astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and immediate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in seed under seed-specific bidirectional promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports astaxanthin biosynthesis in maize; CH module produced 49.23 mg/kg DW astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AdCBFD1 was the first flowering-plant astaxanthin-pathway gene tested in maize engineering. 2. Together with AdHBFD1 it enabled astaxanthin production in seeds. 3. Its output was lower than the algal BZ module but clearly positive.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: when combined with very strong flux diversion to astaxanthin, reduced ABA synthesis was proposed as a possible regeneration issue
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: novel flowering-plant resource for astaxanthin engineering; could be stacked with BZ in tissue-restricted strategies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper notes CH participates in a unique pathway converging at a 4-keto-β-ring intermediate

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: P2R5SGPA seed-specific bidirectional promoter; seed-specific, bidirectional
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression in multigene stacks; comparison among gene-pair combinations; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: carotenoid β-ring 4-hydroxylase family component (HBFD1-associated ketolase route)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: carotenoid biosynthetic enzyme from Adonis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: participates with AdCBFD1 in a higher-plant pathway converting carotene intermediates toward astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and immediate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in seed under seed-specific bidirectional promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports astaxanthin biosynthesis in maize with AdCBFD1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AdHBFD1, together with AdCBFD1, formed a productive flowering-plant module. 2. CH-containing maize accumulated astaxanthin to 49.23 mg/kg DW. 3. The CH route was less effective than the algal BZ route but more effective than the yeast SR route.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: very high diversion of β-carotene away from ABA was discussed as a possible regeneration constraint in strong stacks
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: alternative sink gene resource from flowering plants; useful for combinatorial engineering with spatial control
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: functional assignment described as part of Adonis unique pathway rather than classic CrtZ/BKT route
